title: Hieracium pumilare
intro: Hieracium pumilare, also known as small hawkweed, is a low-growing perennial plant species that belongs to the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium pumilare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium pumilare include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
